scope.goal: Repair the v2176 renderer parity comparison surface after GitHub Actions exposed an unnormalized Markdown path label in the shard readiness contract consumer gate case.

parallelProjects.java: recommended parallel; v2177 changes only Node test normalization and does not require Java to wait
parallelProjects.miniKv: recommended parallel; v2177 changes only Node test normalization and does not require mini-kv to wait

ciFailure.assertion: expected normalized length 7243, received 7263 on GitHub Linux runner
ciFailure.rootCause: The renderer migration normalizer folded JSON path/resolvedPath fields and lower-case path labels, but did not fold Markdown labels rendered as '- Evidence file:' and '- Resolved path:'. The unnormalized absolute runner path changed the comparison length without changing public renderer behavior.

passed.command: npx vitest run test/rendererMigrationV2176Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2167Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2168Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2169Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2170Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2175Parity.test.ts --maxWorkers=2

passed.result: 1 test file passed, 1 test passed under v8 coverage instrumentation; thresholds were set to 0 only because this focused run intentionally does not execute the full suite
2 test files passed, 4 tests passed; v2177 walkthrough counted 3051 Chinese characters and satisfied the current quality gate
passed with 0 errors and 263 existing warnings

safetyBoundary.publicOutput: No public Markdown output was changed. Only normalized test comparison strings changed.
safetyBoundary.testSurface: The normalizer now compares renderer structure and business fields while replacing environment-specific Markdown path labels with stable placeholders.
safetyBoundary.stopCondition: If another CI-only drift appears, inspect the exact output label and add the narrowest normalizer rule; do not change fixtures or renderer output to satisfy parity.
